title: lonicera nigra subsp. barbinervis
intro: Lonicera nigra subsp. barbinervis, also known as Honeysuckle vine, is a woody vine native to North America with fragrant, white, yellow, or red flowers and red berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and alleviating respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Lonicera nigra subsp. barbinervis include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various ailments. The plant's medicinal properties have also led to its use in the production of cough syrups and other pharmaceutical products.
